strengths: Currently building AI-agent infrastructure and developer tooling (MCP frameworks) at re:cinq for ~15 months, demonstrating hands-on engagement with modern agentic patterns and agent orchestration

Extensive real-time systems expertise: built micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby; designed event-driven architecture for GISCollective; demonstrated async I/O and database-scaling capabilities

Consistent commitment to engineering discipline and knowledge-sharing across roles: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, refactoring dojos, open-source maintenance, and workshop teaching signal strong capability to build high-leverage teams
concerns: No demonstrated executive leadership experience: resume lacks CTO/VP titles, clear evidence of scaling engineering teams to required scale, P&L responsibility, or board-level communication
Zero fintech domain expertise: background in geo-data platforms, direct mail optimization, and pricing systems—not credit infrastructure, payment flows, regulatory compliance, or embedded finance

AI systems experience limited to infrastructure and tooling; no demonstrated experience designing end-to-end agentic decision systems operating on financial data with feedback loops or continuous learning

key_factors.factor: Hands-on current work with agentic frameworks and orchestration tooling
Proven expertise in real-time, event-driven distributed systems at scale
No executive leadership track record or fintech domain knowledge

response.justification: Szabo brings genuine technical depth in real-time distributed systems and hands-on experience with agent infrastructure—both core to the architectural challenge. His consistent practice of disciplined engineering (TDD, CI/CD, observability) and mentorship across multiple companies demonstrates capability to build technical culture. Critical gaps remain: no executive experience scaling teams (hard requirement), no fintech regulatory or domain knowledge (credit, compliance, payment infrastructure), and AI experience is infrastructure-focused rather than business-decision-focused. He would be strongest as a senior technical architect or engineering leader reporting to an experienced fintech CTO, rather than in the CTO role itself.
